body.title: [sprint] Repo-map orientation surface: ranked signature map replaces read-to-discover
body.intention: I intend that the coding model sees repo STRUCTURE without reading whole files, because read-to-discover is what makes a weak model wander (47-102 Reads, 0 edits) and bloats context.
body.description:
**Problem:** our loop hands the model generic Read; it discovers structure by reading whole files one at a time, each result (<=3000 chars) re-sent every turn — the read-wander + context-bloat we spent three sessions band-aiding (num_ctx, compaction). aider instead injects a compact (~1k-token) tree-sitter SIGNATURE map of the whole repo (file names, class/method/function signatures, import graph), graph-ranked (files=nodes, dependencies=edges) against the current task, so the model sees structure without file bodies.
**Proposed shape:** build a repo-map orientation surface. PREFER our existing symbols table (memory: symbols-table-is-the-multiplier) if it already carries signatures — do NOT add a redundant tree-sitter dep if the symbols table suffices. Extract signatures, rank by a dependency graph + task keywords (the loop already logs an ORIENTATION/builder_report step — build the map there), budget to N tokens (aider default ~1k), inject into the coding loop's initial context in place of read-to-discover.
**Affected files:** the coding-domain orientation path (agentic_loop.py ORIENTATION / CodingDomain builder_report), a new repo_map module, symbols-table integration. MEDIUM inertia: agentic_loop.py — flag for pre-approval.
**Design rules:** prefer the existing symbols table over a new dep (homogeneity-over-special-case); log the crossing; proof-on-close.
**Scope boundary:** IN — build + rank + token-budget + inject the signature map into coding orientation. OUT — architect/editor split; ACI viewer; changing the edit tools.
**Test plan:** unit test the map builder on a fixture repo — signatures present, file BODIES absent, ranked order puts task-relevant files first, token budget respected. Behavioral red: with the map disabled, orientation falls back to whole-file reads (bodies present / no ranking).
**Completion criteria:** the coding loop's orientation includes a graph-ranked signature map within a token budget AND a test proves structure-without-bodies plus relevance ranking.
**Rollback:** map injection is flagged; disabling restores read-to-discover orientation.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
